steps.title: Trace the underlined role
steps.content: The passage presents Don Jeronimo as a gatekeeper for young playwrights: they need him to get their work produced. The underlined portion then shows how seriously he judges them, welcoming them but criticizing them sharply. Its function is to show that his judgment carries real weight for inexperienced writers.

steps.title: Rule out unrelated functions
steps.content:
• Choice A reverses the point: Don Jeronimo is actively involved with playwrights, not uninterested in supporting them.
  • Choice B is too broad because the underlined portion focuses on how he treats young authors, not on theatrical works in general.
  • Choice C invents a comparison of talent that the passage does not make.
The answer is D.
